title: Eomes expression identifies the early bone marrow precursor to classical NK cells
author: Liang, Zhitao
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository holds the code used for single-cell genomics analysis, including RNA velocity analysis, in Liang, Anderson, et al,  Nature Immunology , 2024 ( doi.org/10.1038/s41590-024-01861-6 ). 
 The RNA velocity analysis code in this repository draws heavily from that of TopicVelo ( doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2306901121 ). Due to the amount of custom code in this project compared to the current TopicVelo release, TopicVelo source code with the custom additions is included in this repository. Most of these additions will also be included in an upcoming TopicVelo release. 
 Single-cell analysis is presented starting from the filtered Seurat object published on GEO. Information on the preprocessing prior to this step, which most notably removed proliferating cells, is present in the Methods section of the accompanying paper and can be discussed further upon request to Hope Anderson at hdanderson@uchicago.edu. 
 A rough analysis pipeline of the code in this repository is as follows: 
 
 The code in  Basic_scRNA_seq_analysis  was performed first to annotate cell types present in the data. 
 Topic modeling was then performed using the code in  Topic_Modeling  
 Last, RNA velocity analysis was performed using the code in  RNA_velocity . 
 
 Please inspect the  README.txt  files in those directories for more details on the file structure. 
  r_session_info.txt  and  topic_velo_env.yml  describe the coding environments used for R and Python to generate the vast majority of these files. 
 The  data  directory includes copies of files presented on GEO, while  analysis  includes intermediates generated from the code in this repository.

 The RNA velocity analysis code in this repository draws heavily from that of TopicVelo ( doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2306901121 ). Due to the amount of custom code in this project compared to the current TopicVelo release, TopicVelo source code with the custom additions is included in this repository. Most of these additions will also be included in an upcoming TopicVelo release. 
 Single-cell analysis is presented starting from the filtered Seurat object published on GEO. Information on the preprocessing prior to this step, which most notably removed proliferating cells, is present in the Methods section of the accompanying paper and can be discussed further upon request to Hope Anderson at hdanderson@uchicago.edu.
